metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_3702
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.3: "NMDA Receptor Stimulation Induces Tiam1 Phosphorylation and Rac1 Activation"
Fig.5: "Reduced Tiam1 Expression Results in Decreased Spine and Synapse Density"
Fig.6: "Effect of Overexpression of Tiam1 Mutants on Spine Density"
Fig.7: "Tiam1 Is Required for NMDA Receptor-Dependent Spine Growth and Intracellular Signaling"

"Tiam1 antibodies were purchased (Santa Cruz) or generated by immunizing rabbits with GST-Tiam1 PDZ (aa 840-1025). Specificity of the Tiam1 antibodies was tested with peptide competition experiments (data not shown) and confirmed with RNAi (Figures 4A and 7B)."

11/12/2017 Pim
- The effect of Tiam1 as a regulator of actin modifications is inferred (by me and the authors) based on the known role of Tiam1 as a Rac1 GEF, the known role of Rac1 in synaptic actin dynamics, the effect of Tiam1 perturbation on Rac1 activation in cultured neurons (Fig.3), and the effect of Tiam1 perturbation on dendritic spine parameters.. Experimental description: "Hippocampal and cortical neurons were prepared from E18 Long-Evans rat embryos (Charles River) as described (Xia et al., 1996)"
